title: pedicularis axillaris var. balfouriana
intro: Pedicularis axillaris var. balfouriana, also known as Balfour's lousewort, is a flowering plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing other plants while still performing photosynthesis. The plant is said to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, Balfour's lousewort is sometimes used to make teas and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues.
